Throwable.generation:
CANGJIE IMPORTS:
```
```

CANGJIE TRANSLATION:
```
Exception
```

REASONING:
In Cangjie, the root class for all errors and exceptions is `Exception`, which is part of the built-in core library and does not require any import. It serves the same role as Java's `Throwable` — it is the superclass of all exception types and can be caught in `catch` clauses. The previous errors occurred because `Throwable` is not a valid type name in Cangjie, and `std.exception` is not a valid package; the correct equivalent is simply `Exception`.

The Java `BufferedReader` type is used for efficient reading of characters, arrays, and lines from a character-input stream. In Cangjie, the equivalent type is also named `BufferedReader` and is provided by the `std.io` module. The import `std.io.*` is necessary to use this type. BufferedReader.prompt:
Use your expertise to translate the given type according to the instructions. Find an equivalent type (corresponding type that maintains the same functionality) in Cangjie by looking into "JAVA TYPE", "JAVA TYPE DESCRIPTION" and "JAVA CODE" as context.

JAVA TYPE:
```
BufferedReader
```

JAVA TYPE DESCRIPTION:
Reads text from a character-input stream, buffering characters so as to
 provide for the efficient reading of characters, arrays, and lines.


JAVA CODE:
```
    private static void printJansiLogoDemo() throws IOException {
        BufferedReader in =
                new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(AnsiMain.class.getResourceAsStream("jansi.txt"), UTF_8));
        try {
            String l;
            while ((l = in.readLine()) != null) {
                System.out.println(l);
            }
        } finally {
            closeQuietly(in);
        }
    }
```

    private static void closeQuietly(Closeable c) {
        try {
            c.close();
        } catch (IOException ioe) {
            ioe.printStackTrace(System.err);
        }
    }
